dd-auth-001.question: How to model authentication state?
dd-auth-001.decision: Two entities: AuthUser (cached profile) and AuthSession (current state with user reference)
dd-auth-001.rationale: Separates user profile data from session state. AuthSession is singleton ('current'), AuthUser can be queried. Follows local state pattern - Supabase owns data, we cache for reactive UI.

dd-auth-002.question: Where do loading/error states belong?
dd-auth-002.rationale: Loading and error are UI concerns, not domain state. Schema tracks who is logged in (domain). useAuth() hook manages loading/error states locally in React.

dd-auth-003.question: How to structure the auth service?
dd-auth-003.decision: IAuthService interface with SupabaseAuthService and MockAuthService implementations
dd-auth-003.rationale: Follows IPersistenceService pattern from analysis findings. Enables testability via DI - inject MockAuthService for unit tests, SupabaseAuthService for production.

dd-auth-004.question: What enhancement hooks will the domain need?
dd-auth-004.decision: enhanceModels: AuthSession.isAuthenticated (view); enhanceCollections: none needed; enhanceRootStore: initializeAuth (action to restore session on app start), syncAuthState (action to update local state from Supabase events)
dd-auth-004.rationale: isAuthenticated computed from user reference presence. initializeAuth restores session on page refresh. syncAuthState handles onAuthStateChange events from Supabase to keep local MST state in sync.
